dags.description: Shared DAG definition for Bedrock Batch Data Processing Pipeline

agent_context.when_to_use: Data enrichment/labeling only — you want Bedrock batch to process text data (classify, extract, summarize) but do NOT need downstream training in the same pipeline.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: If you need training after processing (use bedrock_batch_pytorch_e2e). If data volume is <100K rows (use realtime bedrock_pytorch_e2e).
differentiators: Data processing only — no training node
Cost-efficient batch API (50% cheaper than realtime for >100K rows)
Output goes to S3 for downstream consumption by any pipeline

prerequisites.how: IAM role has bedrock:CreateModelInvocationJob
Input JSONL formatted per Bedrock batch spec

common_pitfalls: Batch input must be JSONL with specific schema — validate before submitting
Batch jobs are async — pipeline must poll for completion
Output needs post-processing to extract structured fields from LLM responses

dags.description: Bedrock Batch-enhanced PyTorch end-to-end pipeline with cost-efficient LLM-based data processing, training, calibration, packaging, registration, and evaluation

agent_context.when_to_use: Need LLM to enrich/label data before PyTorch training AND data volume is >100K rows. Batch API is ~50% cheaper than realtime for large volumes. Used by afn_return_mdr_sopa pattern.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: If data <100K rows (use realtime bedrock_pytorch_e2e — faster turnaround). If you need label ruleset overrides (use bedrock_batch_pytorch_with_label_ruleset_e2e).
differentiators: Batch Bedrock API (async, cost-efficient for large data)
No label ruleset — direct LLM output to training

prerequisites.how: IAM role has bedrock:CreateModelInvocationJob
Template produces parseable structured output for training labels
ml.g5.xlarge+ available in account

common_pitfalls: Batch jobs are async — pipeline wall-clock time is 4-24h
Batch output format differs from realtime — ensure parsing handles JSONL response format
If batch job fails mid-way, partial results may exist in S3 — handle idempotently

dags.description: Bedrock Batch-enhanced PyTorch end-to-end pipeline with label ruleset generation/execution for transparent rule-based label transformation, training, calibration, packaging, and registration

agent_context.when_to_use: LLM labels need rule-based override before training AND data volume is >100K rows. Use when domain experts have deterministic rules (e.g., 'if seller is FBA, always label as low-risk') that should override LLM judgment.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: If no label override rules needed (use bedrock_batch_pytorch_e2e). If data is <100K rows (use realtime variant bedrock_pytorch_with_label_ruleset_e2e).
differentiators: Label ruleset generation + execution (transparent rule-based overrides)

prerequisites.how: IAM role has bedrock:CreateModelInvocationJob
JSON/YAML file with override rules (condition→label mappings)
ml.g5.xlarge+ available in account

common_pitfalls: Label ruleset must be deterministic — non-deterministic rules cause training instability
Ruleset execution order matters — first matching rule wins
Batch jobs are async — total pipeline time is 4-24h depending on data volume

need_label_overrides?.yes: USE THIS (or realtime variant for small data)

dags.description: Shared DAG definition for Bedrock Batch-Enhanced Simple Training Pipeline

agent_context.when_to_use: Quick prototyping of batch Bedrock LLM integration with PyTorch training. Use when you want to test the batch LLM→train flow without calibration/registration overhead.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: Production deployments (no packaging/registration). If you need calibration or model registration, use bedrock_batch_pytorch_e2e.
differentiators: Minimal batch Bedrock pipeline (training only, no registration)

prerequisites.how: IAM role has bedrock:CreateModelInvocationJob permission
Bucket exists and role has read/write access

common_pitfalls: Batch jobs can take hours for large datasets — monitor via CloudWatch
No registration step — must manually promote model artifacts

dags.description: SOPA (Standard Operating Procedure Automation) instruction tuning singleton pipeline. Single-node DAG for fine-tuning a Bedrock LLM on domain-specific SOP instructions.

agent_context.when_to_use: Fine-tune a Bedrock LLM on domain-specific standard operating procedures for automated decision-making. Single-step pipeline without traditional ML calibration or registration.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: If you need classification output (use PyTorch/XGBoost). If zero-shot prompting suffices. If you need calibration/registration (use bedrock_pytorch_e2e variants).

prerequisites.how: JSON/JSONL with instruction-response pairs
Account has foundation model access for customization

common_pitfalls: Dataset format must match Bedrock fine-tuning API requirements
No registration step - model accessed via custom endpoint
Very high cost - validate dataset quality first

dags.description: Bedrock Real-time-enhanced PyTorch end-to-end pipeline with LLM-based data processing, training, calibration, packaging, registration, and evaluation

agent_context.when_to_use: Need LLM to enrich/label data before PyTorch training. Bedrock scores data in real-time, then trains on enriched features.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: If you already have labels (use pytorch/complete_e2e). If data volume is huge (use bedrock_batch variant).

prerequisites.how: IAM role has bedrock:InvokeModel
Know what to ask the LLM about each data point

common_pitfalls: Real-time Bedrock can be slow for >100K rows — consider batch variant
Prompt template must produce parseable structured output

need_llm_labels?.yes: USE THIS (or batch variant for large data)

dags.description: Incremental PyTorch training with Bedrock scoring, EDX anti-join, and EDX upload

agent_context.when_to_use: Incremental retraining: you have a prior EDX scored dataset and need to score only NEW addresses via Bedrock LLM, then retrain on the full growing dataset.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: First-time training (no EDX baseline exists) — use pytorch/complete_e2e or bedrock_pytorch_e2e instead.
differentiators: Only DAG with EDX anti-join (skip already-scored data)
Requires prior EDX upload to exist

prerequisites.check: EDX scored manifest exists from prior run
prerequisites.how: Verify EDX ARN accessible via Cradle
IAM role has bedrock:InvokeModel for target model
Cradle date range covers period NOT in existing EDX

common_pitfalls: EDX read ARN must point to PREVIOUS batch output, not the one being written (use separate _CRADLE_EDX_END vs _UPLOAD_EDX_END)
MDS date range must NOT overlap scored EDX period — causes empty anti-join result
pandas str.split("|||") uses regex — must pass regex=False or use re.escape()
ml.g5.12xlarge ModelInference is NVMe — VolumeKmsKeyId handled by compiler

dags.description: Two-pipeline pattern: LLM inference uploads to Andes, training consumes from EDX

agent_context.when_to_use: Two-pipeline architecture: a separate LLM inference pipeline uploads scored labels to EDX/Andes, and THIS pipeline trains from that uploaded data. Use when inference and training have different schedules or ownership.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: If LLM scoring and training run in the same pipeline (use bedrock_batch_pytorch_e2e). If no EDX round-trip is needed (use pytorch/complete_e2e).
differentiators: Training pipeline only — expects LLM labels already in EDX
CradleDataLoading reads from EDX (not raw MDS)

prerequisites.check: EDX dataset with LLM-scored labels exists
prerequisites.how: Verify EDX ARN accessible via Cradle query
Check EDX upload timestamps for freshness
ml.g5.xlarge+ available in account

common_pitfalls: Cradle SQL must point to EDX-uploaded data, not raw MDS
If inference pipeline hasn't run, training data will be stale or empty
Data upload pipeline and training pipeline must agree on column schema

dags.description: Bedrock Real-time-enhanced PyTorch end-to-end pipeline with label ruleset generation/execution for transparent rule-based label transformation, training, calibration, packaging, and registration

agent_context.when_to_use: LLM labels need rule-based override before training AND data volume is <100K rows. Realtime API gives faster turnaround. Used by rnr_pytorch_bedrock pattern for smaller datasets.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: If data volume >100K rows (use batch variant — bedrock_batch_pytorch_with_label_ruleset_e2e). If no label override rules needed (use bedrock_pytorch_e2e).
differentiators: Real-time Bedrock (synchronous, faster for small data)
Label ruleset generation + execution (transparent rule-based overrides)
Faster iteration cycle than batch variant

prerequisites.how: IAM role has bedrock:InvokeModel
JSON/YAML file with override rules (condition→label mappings)
ml.g5.xlarge+ available in account

common_pitfalls: Real-time API has throttling limits — may hit TPS caps on large data
Label ruleset must be deterministic — non-deterministic rules cause training instability
Slower and more expensive than batch for >100K rows

need_label_overrides?.yes: USE THIS (or batch variant for large data)

dags.description: Shared DAG definition for Bedrock-Enhanced Simple Training Pipeline

agent_context.when_to_use: Quick prototyping of realtime Bedrock LLM integration with PyTorch training. Minimal pipeline to test LLM→train flow without calibration/registration.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: Production deployments (no packaging/registration). If you need full E2E pipeline, use bedrock_pytorch_e2e. If data >100K rows, use batch variant.
differentiators: Minimal realtime Bedrock pipeline (training only, no registration)
Fastest way to test Bedrock→PyTorch integration

prerequisites.how: IAM role has bedrock:InvokeModel
DummyDataLoading generates synthetic data or S3 path configured

common_pitfalls: Realtime API can be slow for large datasets — only use for prototyping
No registration step — must manually promote model artifacts
No calibration — scores may not be well-calibrated

dags.description: Basic end-to-end pipeline with dummy training, packaging, payload preparation, and registration

agent_context.when_to_use: Testing/development only: validate the packaging→registration pipeline flow without real training. Simplest way to test MIMS registration works.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: ANY production use. Never use dummy DAGs for real model deployment.
differentiators: No data loading required — DummyTraining generates mock model

prerequisites.how: Role has MIMS permissions
Confirm this is for testing, not production

safe_defaults: All defaults are fine for testing
common_pitfalls: NEVER deploy a model from a dummy DAG to production
Registration will create real MIMS entries — clean up after testing
Use a test service_name to avoid polluting production registries

dags.description: Dummy training pipeline with inference, metrics computation, and wiki generation

agent_context.when_to_use: Testing/development only: validate the inference→metrics→wiki pipeline flow without real training. Tests ModelMetricsComputation and ModelWikiGenerator integration.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: ANY production use. Never use dummy DAGs for real model training or deployment.
differentiators: Tests inference + metrics + wiki generation flow
DummyTraining produces a mock model (no real learning)
No real data required — uses DummyDataLoading

prerequisites.how: Confirm this is for testing, not production

safe_defaults: All defaults are fine for testing
common_pitfalls: NEVER deploy a model from a dummy DAG
Metrics will be meaningless — only validates pipeline plumbing
Wiki content will be generated but based on synthetic metrics

dags.description: Complete LightGBM end-to-end pipeline with training, calibration, packaging, registration, and evaluation

agent_context.when_to_use: Tabular classification/regression with structured features. LightGBM is faster than XGBoost, handles categorical features natively, and requires no GPU. Used by abuse_polygraph pattern.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: If data is primarily text (use PyTorch). If you need feature selection or stratified sampling (use XGBoost variants with those features). If multi-task learning needed (use mtl/ DAGs).
differentiators: LightGBM handles categoricals natively (no one-hot encoding required)
Faster training than XGBoost (histogram-based, leaf-wise growth)
Simpler than XGBoost complete_e2e (fewer preprocessing options)

prerequisites.how: Cradle SQL returns rows with label column and numeric/categorical features
ml.m5.xlarge available in account

common_pitfalls: Categorical features must be declared in config — LightGBM won't auto-detect
num_leaves too high causes overfitting (keep ≤ 2^max_depth)
Class imbalance — set scale_pos_weight or use is_unbalance=true

tabular_data?.yes: USE THIS (or XGBoost if team prefers)

dags.description: Complete LightGBM end-to-end pipeline with training, percentile model calibration path, testing path (no calibration), packaging, registration, inference, metrics computation, and wiki generation

agent_context.when_to_use: Tabular classification where you need percentile-based calibration (map raw scores to percentile ranks) AND automated model wiki documentation. Used by almond_pfw, pfw_mtgbm_na patterns.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: If isotonic/Platt calibration is sufficient (use lightgbm/complete_e2e). If you don't need wiki documentation or separate testing path.
differentiators: Three data splits: training, calibration, testing
Testing path uses raw model (no calibration) for true performance metrics

prerequisites.how: Cradle SQL can provide training, calibration, and testing data (different date ranges or splits)
ml.m5.xlarge available in account
ModelWikiGenerator config specifies wiki space and template

common_pitfalls: Testing data must NOT overlap with training or calibration data
Percentile calibration requires sufficient calibration data volume (>10K rows recommended)
Wiki generation may fail silently if wiki space doesn't exist

dags.description: Complete LightGBMMT multi-task end-to-end pipeline with training, calibration, packaging, registration, and evaluation

agent_context.when_to_use: Multi-task learning: predict multiple related labels simultaneously (e.g., fraud type + severity). Shared representation across tasks.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: Single binary classification (use XGBoost or PyTorch). Unrelated tasks (train separately).

prerequisites.how: Data has 2+ label columns that benefit from joint training
Structured data, not raw text

common_pitfalls: Tasks must be correlated for MTL benefit — independent tasks train better separately

text_input?.yes: No MTL for text yet — use PyTorch with multi-head

dags.description: LightGBMMT multi-task end-to-end pipeline with label ruleset generation/execution for transparent rule-based label transformation, training, calibration, packaging, and registration

agent_context.when_to_use: Multi-task tabular classification with deterministic label override rules. Use when you have multiple related binary labels on the same data AND domain experts have rules to override some labels (e.g., 'if condition X, always set task_Y=1'). Used by almond_pfw pattern.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: If no label override rules needed (use mtl/complete_e2e). If single-task (use lightgbm/complete_e2e). If you need semi-supervised learning (use mtl/ssl_training).
differentiators: Label ruleset generation + execution (transparent rule-based overrides)
Handles correlated tasks better than independent models

prerequisites.how: Cradle SQL returns rows with multiple label columns
JSON/YAML with override rules per task
ml.m5.xlarge available in account

common_pitfalls: Label ruleset must cover ALL tasks — partial rules may leave some tasks un-overridden
Multi-task calibration produces separate calibration per task — ensure enough calibration data per task
Rule conflicts between tasks must be resolved in ruleset config

dags.description: LightGBMMT multi-task Semi-Supervised Learning pipeline with pretraining, pseudo-labeling, active sampling, merge, and fine-tuning

agent_context.when_to_use: Multi-task tabular learning when you have limited labeled data but abundant unlabeled data. Two-stage: pretrain on labeled, pseudo-label unlabeled via active sample selection, fine-tune on merged dataset. Used by pfw_mtgbm_na pattern.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: If you have sufficient labeled data for all tasks (use mtl/complete_e2e). If single-task (use xgboost/ssl_training). If you need label override rules (use mtl/lightgbmmt_with_label_ruleset_e2e).
differentiators: Semi-supervised: leverages unlabeled data via pseudo-labeling
Two-stage training: pretrain → active sampling → pseudo-label merge → fine-tune

prerequisites.how: Cradle SQL returns labeled rows (can be small)
Cradle SQL returns unlabeled rows for pseudo-labeling
Separate Cradle query for calibration split
ml.m5.xlarge available (trains twice: pretrain + finetune)

common_pitfalls: Confidence threshold too low → noisy pseudo-labels degrade model
Confidence threshold too high → too few pseudo-labels to benefit
Unlabeled data must be from same distribution as labeled data
Pipeline trains TWICE (pretrain + finetune) — double the compute time

dags.description: Shared DAG definition for PyTorch Complete End-to-End Pipeline

agent_context.when_to_use: Standard PyTorch text classification: have labeled data in Cradle/MDS, want full training + calibration + registration pipeline.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: If data is tabular-only (use XGBoost). If you need LLM labeling (use bedrock_pytorch_e2e).

prerequisites.how: Cradle SQL returns rows with label column
ml.g5.xlarge+ available in account

common_pitfalls: Ensure text column preprocessing matches inference handler

dags.description: Shared DAG definition for PyTorch Complete End-to-End Pipeline with Dummy Data Loading

agent_context.when_to_use: Integration testing of the full PyTorch E2E pipeline (train+calibrate+package+register) WITHOUT needing real Cradle/MDS data. Uses DummyDataLoading to generate synthetic data.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: Production training (use pytorch/complete_e2e with real data). If you don't need calibration/registration (use pytorch/simple_training).
differentiators: Full E2E pipeline but with DummyDataLoading (no Cradle dependency)
Tests packaging and registration flow with synthetic data
Same structure as complete_e2e — easy to swap to real data

prerequisites.how: ml.g5.xlarge available in account
Role has MIMS permissions (for registration step)

common_pitfalls: Registration will create real model artifacts — clean up after testing
Dummy data distribution won't match production — don't use for model validation
Calibration on dummy data is meaningless — only tests the pipeline flow

dags.description: Two-stage hybrid pipeline: PyTorch encoder (Stage-1) produces embeddings, then XGBoost (Stage-2) stacks on [tabular | embedding]. Calibration path threads through both stages.

agent_context.when_to_use: Combine deep learning text/multimodal embeddings with tabular features via XGBoost stacking. Best for complex decision problems needing both text semantics and structured signals.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: If text-only classification suffices (use standard PyTorch DAG). If tabular-only (use XGBoost DAG). If you don't need two-stage complexity.
differentiators: Two-stage: PyTorch encoder -> embedding -> XGBoost stack
Most complex PyTorch pipeline: 13 nodes, 16 edges

prerequisites.how: Cradle SQL returns text AND numeric/categorical features
ml.g5.xlarge+ available

common_pitfalls: Embedding dim must match between PyTorchTraining and XGBoostTraining
Package step takes XGBoostTraining output, not PyTorchTraining

dags.description: Shared DAG definition for Simple Training Pipeline

agent_context.when_to_use: Quick prototyping of PyTorch model training with dummy/synthetic data. Minimal pipeline to validate model architecture and training loop.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: Production deployments (no calibration/packaging/registration). If you need full E2E, use pytorch/complete_e2e or pytorch/standard_e2e.
differentiators: Simplest possible PyTorch pipeline (3 nodes only)

prerequisites.how: ml.g5.xlarge available in account

common_pitfalls: No registration — must manually promote artifacts for downstream use
Dummy data may not represent production distribution

dags.description: Shared DAG definition for PyTorch Standard End-to-End Pipeline

agent_context.when_to_use: Standard PyTorch E2E when you want training + packaging + registration but WITHOUT calibration. Model scores go directly to registration. Used by names3risk_pytorch pattern.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: If you need calibration (use pytorch/complete_e2e). If just prototyping (use pytorch/simple_training).
differentiators: No calibration step — faster pipeline, simpler
Lighter than complete_e2e (no calibration split needed)

prerequisites.how: Cradle SQL returns rows with label column
Separate Cradle query for validation split
ml.g5.xlarge+ available in account

common_pitfalls: No calibration — raw model scores may not be well-calibrated probabilities
If downstream consumers expect calibrated scores, use complete_e2e instead
Validation data should be from a different time period than training

dags.description: PyTorch end-to-end pipeline with StratifiedSampling for class-balanced training and PercentileCalibration for rank-ordered scoring

agent_context.when_to_use: PyTorch text classification with imbalanced labels (need stratified sampling) and percentile-based calibration for rank-ordered output scores.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: If labels are balanced (use standard complete_e2e). If standard isotonic calibration suffices. If no text features (use XGBoost).
differentiators: StratifiedSampling before training for class balance

prerequisites.how: Check class distribution in training SQL
ml.g5.xlarge+ available

common_pitfalls: Calibration data should NOT be stratified - use natural distribution

dags.description: PyTorch end-to-end pipeline with custom TokenizerTraining step and PercentileCalibration. Trains a domain-specific tokenizer before model training.

agent_context.when_to_use: When you need a domain-specific tokenizer trained on your corpus before model training. Ideal for specialized vocabularies (names, addresses, codes) where pretrained tokenizers perform poorly.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: If pretrained tokenizer (BERT, LaBSE) works well (use standard complete_e2e). If no text features (use XGBoost).
differentiators: TokenizerTraining builds custom vocabulary from training data
PyTorchTraining receives BOTH tokenizer and preprocessed tabular data
Best for domain-specific text (names, codes, short-strings)

prerequisites.how: Standard BERT tokenizer produces excessive UNK tokens
ml.g5.xlarge+ available

common_pitfalls: Tokenizer must be packaged with model for inference
Calibration data uses the TRAINED tokenizer

dags.description: Shared DAG definition for PyTorch training pipeline.

agent_context.when_to_use: Training + validation only — when you want to train and evaluate a model but handle packaging/registration separately (e.g., manual promotion, or a separate deployment pipeline).
agent_context.when_not_to_use: If you need automated registration (use pytorch/standard_e2e or pytorch/complete_e2e). If just prototyping without Cradle data (use pytorch/simple_training).
differentiators: Good for iterative model development before committing to registration

prerequisites.how: Cradle SQL returns rows with label column
Separate Cradle query for validation split
ml.g5.xlarge available in account

common_pitfalls: No registration — model artifacts stay in S3, must be promoted manually
Validation split should be temporal (not random) for production models
If model looks good, graduate to standard_e2e or complete_e2e for production

iterating_on_model?.yes: USE THIS DAG (fast iteration, no registration overhead)

dags.description: TSA (Temporal Self-Attention) complete end-to-end pipeline with TSA-specific preprocessing, separate testing and calibration paths, and model calibration.

agent_context.when_to_use: TSA-specific pipelines with custom TSAPreprocessing and TSATabularPreprocessing. Three separate data paths (training, testing, calibration) with TSA-specific preprocessing sharing learned transformations from training.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: If standard tabular preprocessing suffices (use standard PyTorch/XGBoost). If no time-series aggregation needed. If simpler pipeline suffices.
differentiators: TSAPreprocessing steps in all three data paths

prerequisites.how: Time-series aggregate features from Cradle
ml.g5.xlarge+ available

common_pitfalls: TSATabularPreprocessing output must feed into BOTH testing and calibration TSAPreprocessing
Three separate Cradle data loads required

dags.description: Singleton pipeline for CradleDataLoading training data step

agent_context.when_to_use: Standalone data loading step: validate Cradle SQL query, test MDS connectivity, or extract data for offline analysis. Single-node pipeline for data exploration.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: If you need training or any downstream processing (use a full pipeline DAG). This is purely for data extraction/validation.
differentiators: Single node — smallest possible Cursus pipeline
Output can be inspected before committing to a full pipeline

prerequisites.how: Role has Cradle read permissions and MDS dataset exists

common_pitfalls: Output goes to S3 but no downstream consumer — must check artifacts manually
Large queries may timeout — add date range filters to Cradle SQL
Not meant for production pipelines — use as debugging/exploration tool

dags.description: Complete XGBoost end-to-end pipeline with training, calibration, packaging, registration, and evaluation

agent_context.when_to_use: Tabular binary/multiclass classification with XGBoost. Standard path for structured data with numeric/categorical features.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: If input is raw text (use PyTorch). If multi-task output needed (use MTL/LightGBMMT).

prerequisites.how: Data has numeric/categorical columns, not raw text
Binary or multiclass integer labels

common_pitfalls: Feature columns must match between training and inference
Calibration data should be from a different time period than training

dags.description: Complete XGBoost end-to-end pipeline with dummy data loading, training, calibration, packaging, registration, and evaluation

agent_context.when_to_use: End-to-end pipeline testing without real data. Validates full pipeline mechanics including packaging and registration steps using synthetic data. Used for CI/CD, integration testing, and demonstrating complete workflow.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: Never for production models. If you have real data (use complete_e2e). For testing purposes only.
differentiators: DummyDataLoading instead of Cradle for both training and calibration
Full e2e structure: train, calibrate, package, register
Tests registration mechanics without real model
CI/CD friendly - no external data dependencies

prerequisites.how: Testing full pipeline flow including registration
Test/dev MIMS endpoint for registration validation

common_pitfalls: Registered models from dummy data must be deregistered after testing
Do not promote dummy-trained models to production
Test MIMS endpoint may have different permissions than prod

dags.description: Complete XGBoost end-to-end pipeline with training, percentile model calibration path, testing path (no calibration), packaging, registration, inference, metrics computation, and wiki generation

agent_context.when_to_use: Full production pipeline when you need percentile-based calibration (rank-ordering scores into percentile buckets) plus a separate testing path for unbiased evaluation, wiki generation, and model registration. Used by PDA EU/NA, CAP DNR EU projects that need percentile-based risk scores.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: If standard isotonic/Platt calibration suffices (use complete_e2e). If you don't need separate testing path (use complete_e2e_with_wiki). If you don't need registration (use training_with_calibration).
differentiators: Three separate data paths: training, calibration, testing
Testing path produces unbiased metrics (no calibration applied)

prerequisites.how: Score bucket boundaries (e.g., P10, P25, P50, P75, P90) agreed with stakeholders
Training SQL, calibration SQL, testing SQL from different time periods
Service registered in MIMS with valid service_name

common_pitfalls: Testing data must NOT be used in calibration - it provides unbiased evaluation
Percentile calibration is monotonic but not probability-calibrated
Wiki generation may fail if metrics format doesn't match template

need_wiki?.no: Use complete_e2e with percentile calibration step only

dags.description: Complete XGBoost end-to-end pipeline with training, calibration path, testing path (no calibration), packaging, registration, inference, metrics computation, and wiki generation

agent_context.when_to_use: Full production pipeline with separate testing path for unbiased model evaluation, plus percentile calibration, wiki generation, and registration. Used when you need both deployment AND rigorous out-of-sample testing metrics. Similar to complete_e2e_with_percentile_calibration but the canonical 'testing' variant.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: If you don't need separate testing (use complete_e2e_with_wiki or complete_e2e). If standard calibration is fine (use complete_e2e). If you don't need registration (use training_with_evaluation).
differentiators: Three data paths: training, calibration, testing
Testing path is independent - no calibration applied for honest metrics
Same structure as complete_e2e_with_percentile_calibration (this is the standard name for that pattern)

prerequisites.how: Training, calibration, and testing SQLs from non-overlapping periods
Valid service_name in MIMS for model registration
Metrics output format matches wiki generator expectations

common_pitfalls: Testing data temporal leakage will give optimistic metrics
Wiki generation requires specific metrics format
Three datasets means 3x data loading time - budget accordingly

need_wiki_documentation?.no: Use complete_e2e and add manual evaluation

dags.description: Complete XGBoost end-to-end pipeline with training, calibration, packaging, registration, inference, metrics computation, and wiki generation

agent_context.when_to_use: Full production pipeline with model wiki documentation generation. Used when team needs automated model documentation for model review boards or compliance. Includes calibration, metrics computation, wiki generation, and registration.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: If you don't need wiki/documentation (use complete_e2e). If you need separate testing path for unbiased metrics (use complete_e2e_with_testing). If you don't need registration (use training_with_calibration).
differentiators: No separate testing path - metrics from calibration data

prerequisites.how: Model wiki format agreed with review board
Valid service_name for model registration
Separate time period data for calibration and metrics

common_pitfalls: Wiki metrics come from calibration data, not independent test set
For unbiased metrics reporting, use complete_e2e_with_testing instead
Wiki generation failure does not block registration

dags.description: XGBoost end-to-end pipeline with PercentileModelCalibration (rank-ordered scoring). Minimal calibration path without separate testing or wiki generation.

agent_context.when_to_use: Standard XGBoost pipeline with percentile-based calibration for rank-ordered scores. Simplest percentile calibration path without separate testing or wiki.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: If you need separate testing evaluation (use complete_e2e_with_percentile_calibration). If standard isotonic calibration suffices (use complete_e2e). If you need wiki (use with_percentile_calibration variant).
differentiators: No separate testing or wiki generation

prerequisites.how: Cradle SQL returns rows with label and feature columns
Service registered in MIMS

common_pitfalls: Uses XGBoostModelEval (not XGBoostModelInference) for calibration
No separate test set - calibration data serves dual purpose

dags.description: Simple XGBoost training pipeline with data loading and preprocessing

agent_context.when_to_use: Quick prototyping or experimentation where you only need to train an XGBoost model without registration or deployment. Good for initial model development and hyperparameter exploration.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: If you need model registration (use complete_e2e). If you need calibrated scores (use training_with_calibration). If you need evaluation metrics (use training_with_evaluation).
differentiators: Minimal pipeline - just load, preprocess, train

prerequisites.how: Verify cradle_sql query returns tabular data with label column
List of numeric/categorical feature names available

common_pitfalls: No evaluation means you cannot assess model quality in-pipeline
Model artifact is not registered - manual steps needed for deployment

dags.description: XGBoost Semi-Supervised Learning pipeline with pretraining, pseudo-labeling, active sampling, merge, and fine-tuning

agent_context.when_to_use: When you have limited labeled data but abundant unlabeled data and want to leverage semi-supervised learning. Uses pretrain->pseudo-label->active-sample->merge->finetune pattern. Used by teams exploring SSL for abuse detection where labeled abuse cases are scarce.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: If you have sufficient labeled data (use complete_e2e or training variants). If unlabeled data quality is poor (SSL amplifies noise). If model interpretability is critical (pseudo-labeling adds opacity).
differentiators: Two-stage training: pretrain on labeled, finetune on labeled+pseudo
PseudoLabelMerge combines original labels with pseudo-labels

prerequisites.how: Labeled dataset too small for good supervised performance (<10K labeled samples)
Unlabeled pool at least 5-10x size of labeled set
Cradle SQL for unlabeled data pool
Valid service_name for model registration

common_pitfalls: Low confidence threshold introduces noisy pseudo-labels
SSL can hurt performance if unlabeled data distribution differs from labeled
Always compare against supervised baseline before committing to SSL
Active sampling threshold is critical - too aggressive loses signal

labeled_data_insufficient?.no: Use complete_e2e (supervised is better with enough labels)

unlabeled_data_available?.no: Cannot use SSL - use training_with_stratified for imbalanced supervised

dags.description: XGBoost training pipeline with model calibration

agent_context.when_to_use: When you need calibrated probability scores (e.g., for threshold-based decisioning) but don't need model registration. Used by teams like PDA, CAP who need calibrated outputs for risk scoring.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: If you need model registration (use complete_e2e). If you need percentile-based calibration (use complete_e2e_with_percentile_calibration). If calibration is not needed (use simple_dag or training_with_evaluation).
differentiators: Calibration uses separate holdout data split

prerequisites.how: Cradle SQL for calibration data from different time period than training
Check common_configs/standard_calibration_dictionary.json for score bucket mappings

common_pitfalls: Calibration data MUST be from a different time period than training data
Using same data for train and calibrate leads to overfitting calibration

dags.description: XGBoost training pipeline with feature selection and model calibration

agent_context.when_to_use: When you need both feature selection (to reduce dimensionality/remove noisy features) AND calibrated scores, but don't need full e2e registration. Useful for models with many candidate features where automatic pruning improves generalization.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: If you don't need feature selection (use training_with_calibration). If you need full e2e with registration (use complete_e2e). If you need preprocessing like missing value imputation (use training_with_feature_selection which includes MVI+risk tables).
differentiators: Combines FeatureSelection + Calibration in one DAG
Feature selection applied to both training and calibration branches
No missing value imputation or risk table mapping (unlike training_with_feature_selection)

prerequisites.check: Large feature set that needs pruning
prerequisites.how: More than 50 candidate features where many may be irrelevant
Cradle SQL for calibration data from different time period

common_pitfalls: Feature selection threshold too aggressive can remove useful features
Feature selection model uses same training data - ensure calibration data uses selected features

dags.description: XGBoost training pipeline with model evaluation

agent_context.when_to_use: When you need to evaluate model quality (AUC, precision, recall) on a holdout set but don't need calibration or registration. Good for model comparison experiments and reporting metrics.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: If you need calibrated scores (use training_with_calibration). If you need production deployment (use complete_e2e). If you need stratified sampling for imbalanced data (use training_with_stratified).
differentiators: Adds XGBoostModelEval step with separate evaluation data

prerequisites.how: Cradle SQL for eval data, ideally from different time period
Know which metrics matter (AUC, precision@k, recall@k)

common_pitfalls: Eval data must not overlap with training data
Time-based splits preferred over random splits for abuse detection

dags.description: XGBoost training pipeline with model evaluation using dummy data loading

agent_context.when_to_use: Testing and development only. Uses DummyDataLoading instead of Cradle to generate synthetic data for pipeline validation without needing real data access. Used for CI/CD testing, pipeline debugging, and onboarding demos.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: Never use for production model training. If you have real data access (use training_with_evaluation). For demo purposes only.
differentiators: No Cradle/MDS dependency - runs without data access
Used for pipeline testing and CI/CD validation

prerequisites.how: You are testing pipeline mechanics, not training a real model
Synthetic data sufficient for your testing purpose

common_pitfalls: Models trained on dummy data have no real-world validity
Do not register or deploy models from this DAG
Dummy data may not expose real preprocessing issues

have_data_access?.no: USE THIS DAG for validation only

dags.description: XGBoost training pipeline with feature selection and model evaluation

agent_context.when_to_use: When you have many features and need automated feature importance filtering, plus missing value imputation and risk table mapping. Used by teams like active_queueing_pfoc, active_queueing_mfnri that have 100+ features from variable catalogs and need data preprocessing.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: If you don't have missing values or categorical risk features (use training_with_evaluation). If you need registration (use complete_e2e). If you only need feature selection without MVI/risk tables (use training_with_calibration_fs).
differentiators: Artifacts from training preprocessing are reused for evaluation branch
Used by active_queueing and regional_xgboost projects with large variable catalogs

prerequisites.how: Data has NULL/NaN values requiring imputation strategy
High-cardinality categoricals that benefit from risk table mapping
50+ candidate features where pruning adds value

common_pitfalls: Imputation artifacts must be saved and reused at inference time
Risk table mappings must be versioned with the model
Feature selection reduces feature count - ensure downstream compatibility

need_risk_table_encoding?.no: Use training_with_calibration_fs for just feature selection

dags.description: XGBoost training pipeline with advanced preprocessing (missing value imputation and risk table mapping) and model evaluation

agent_context.when_to_use: When you need missing value imputation and risk table mapping but NOT feature selection. Used when feature set is already curated but data quality requires preprocessing. Similar to training_with_feature_selection but without the feature pruning step.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: If you also need feature selection (use training_with_feature_selection). If data is already clean (use training_with_evaluation). If you need registration (use complete_e2e).
differentiators: Preprocessing artifacts shared between training and evaluation branches
Good when feature set is known-good but data has quality issues

prerequisites.check: Features with missing values requiring imputation
prerequisites.how: Check data for NULL rates per feature
Identify high-cardinality categoricals for risk table mapping
No need for automated feature pruning

common_pitfalls: Imputation and risk table artifacts must travel with model to inference
Risk table mapping on calibration data must use training-derived tables
Missing value patterns in prod must match training

dags.description: XGBoost training pipeline with stratified sampling and model evaluation

agent_context.when_to_use: When training data has severe class imbalance (e.g., <5% positive rate) and stratified sampling is needed to balance classes for better model learning. Common in abuse detection where abusers are a small fraction of all buyers.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: If class balance is acceptable (>10% minority class), use training_with_evaluation. If you need full e2e pipeline (use complete_e2e with stratified sampling configured). If using scale_pos_weight in XGBoost instead of data-level sampling.
differentiators: StratifiedSampling step between preprocessing and training
Handles class imbalance at data level rather than algorithm level
Evaluation uses original (non-stratified) data for realistic metrics
Used when scale_pos_weight alone is insufficient

prerequisites.how: Positive rate < 5% in training data
At least 1000 positive samples in training set

common_pitfalls: Do NOT stratify evaluation data - it must reflect real distribution
Over-sampling can cause overfitting to minority class
Consider scale_pos_weight as alternative before using this DAG

tried_scale_pos_weight?.yes: USE THIS DAG if still underperforming

need_registration?.yes: Use complete_e2e with stratified sampling configured

dags.description: LightGBMMT multi-task end-to-end pipeline with TEMPORAL-SPLIT preprocessing (time-based train split), dual evaluation (testing + calibration paths), model calibration, packaging, and registration.

agent_context.when_to_use: Multi-task learning on time-series/temporal data where the train/test boundary MUST be split chronologically (no future data leaks into training). Predict multiple related labels simultaneously with a time-ordered split and a dedicated held-out testing evaluation path.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: Non-temporal data where a random split is appropriate (use mtl/complete_e2e). Single binary classification (use xgboost or pytorch). Unrelated tasks (train separately).
differentiators: TemporalSplitPreprocessing on the training path (time-ordered split) instead of TabularPreprocessing — prevents temporal leakage
Separate LightGBMMTModelEval_testing path (held-out chronological test) in addition to the calibration eval path
Dual evaluation: testing (from the temporal split) + calibration (from a separate Cradle query)
Multi-task output (multiple labels) with shared feature representation

prerequisites.how: Data has a timestamp/date column to define the chronological train/test boundary
Data has 2+ label columns that benefit from joint training
Structured data, not raw text

common_pitfalls: Temporal split boundary must be set so the test window has enough data for meaningful evaluation
Do not shuffle across the temporal boundary — that reintroduces leakage
Tasks must be correlated for MTL benefit — independent tasks train better separately

temporal_data?.yes: USE THIS DAG (chronological split prevents leakage)

text_input?.yes: No MTL for text yet — use PyTorch with multi-head

dags.description: XGBoost multi-task end-to-end pipeline with TEMPORAL-SPLIT preprocessing (time-based train split), dual evaluation (testing + calibration paths), model calibration, packaging, and registration.

agent_context.when_to_use: XGBoost multi-task learning (one_output_per_tree) on time-series/temporal data where the train/test boundary MUST be split chronologically (no future data leaks into training). Predict multiple related labels simultaneously with a time-ordered split and a dedicated held-out testing evaluation path.
agent_context.when_not_to_use: Non-temporal data where a random split is appropriate. Single-task XGBoost (use xgboost/complete_e2e). Unrelated tasks (train separately). LightGBM-family MTL (use mtl/temporal_split_e2e).
differentiators: XgboostMt framework — multi-task XGBoost (one_output_per_tree) predicting multiple labels simultaneously
TemporalSplitPreprocessing on the training path (time-ordered split) instead of TabularPreprocessing — prevents temporal leakage
Separate XgboostMtModelEval_testing path (held-out chronological test) in addition to the calibration eval path
Dual evaluation: testing (from the temporal split) + calibration (from a separate Cradle query)

prerequisites.how: Data has a timestamp/date column to define the chronological train/test boundary
Data has 2+ label columns that benefit from joint training
Structured data, not raw text

common_pitfalls: Temporal split boundary must be set so the test window has enough data for meaningful evaluation
Do not shuffle across the temporal boundary — that reintroduces leakage
Tasks must be correlated for MTL benefit — independent tasks train better separately

temporal_data?.yes: USE THIS DAG (chronological split prevents leakage)
temporal_data?.no: Use xgboost/complete_e2e (single-task) or a random-split MTL DAG
